title: pilosella officinarum subsp. velutifolia
intro: Pilosella officinarum subsp. velutifolia, also known as Velvet-leaved Mouse-ear or Velvet-leaved Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its woolly leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Pilosella officinarum subsp. velutifolia have been reported to offer various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
